vulnerability.reference: Developers rapidly adapting tasks using compressed in-context examples without validating denoised outputs.
vulnerability.generated: Unsupervised self-training without ground-truth validation blinds developers to hidden visual error amplification.

impact.reference: Cascading errors in downstream applications due to corrupted example-based learning.
impact.generated: Deployed models produce unreliable predictions in OOD scenarios, eroding trust in LMM-based adaptation and causing downstream task failures.

dose_response.reference: In-context denoising on compressed examples may amplify errors, enabling corruption of learned behaviors, which exposes developers to flawed models; combined with skipped validation, this leads to cascading downstream errors.
dose_response.generated: In-context denoising via self-training transition matrices amplifies hidden visual errors in MLLMs, through LMM-based task adaptation, triggers unsupervised self-training without ground-truth validation, blinding developers to error amplification, leading to unreliable OOD predictions, eroding trust and causing downstream failures.
